title: Bitter Silky Brazilian Fish Stew
description: A velvety fish stew with sharp pepper notes, perfect for a romantic dinner that keeps sugar low and flavors bright.

ingredients.name: firm white fish fillets (cod or snapper)

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Incorporate the grated ginger and chopped chili, cooking for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the sliced bell peppers and diced tomato, cooking for 5 minutes until they soften.
Add the cubed fish, coconut milk, and fish stock; stir to combine.
Season with 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp sea salt, and 1/2 tsp black pepper, mixing well.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer gently for 10-12 minutes, or until the fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Finish the stew with a splash of lime juice, adjusting seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.
Enjoy your silky, bitter Brazilian fish stew!
